text:
If you were hoping today would clear out the smoke haze, hold that thought for Sunday. Air quality is flagged unhealthy for sensitive groups again this afternoon as wildfire smoke drifts back in, layered right on top of a soggy, unsettled day. Showers are likely from late morning on, with thunderstorms firing up between about 2 and 5pm and lingering into the evening, high near 81. The models are arguing about just how wet: the American model keeps it fairly modest, but the European and Canadian guidance both go all in on a washout, so I'd plan around getting soaked, not just sprinkled on. The lake's under a wind advisory too, south winds building into the 15 to 25 knot range with waves 1 to 3 feet, so pleasure boaters should be off the broad waters well before those afternoon storms arrive. Tonight's rain continues into the early morning hours before clearing out behind a west wind shift.

Sunday flips the script entirely, mostly sunny, high near 77, lighter winds, and better air. If you've got outdoor plans, save them for tomorrow.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-18T10:35:00+00:00): Cloudy, 64F feels 64F, humidity 64%, wind SSE 6 mph
ALERTS: Air Quality Alert issued July 17 at 3:27PM EDT by NWS Burlington VT
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Rain showers likely before 1pm, then rain showers likely and patchy smoke between 1pm and 2pm, then patchy smoke and showers and thunderstorms likely between 2pm and 4pm, then areas of smoke and a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 4pm and 5pm, then patchy smoke and showers and thunderstorms likely. Mostly cloudy, with a high near 81. South wind 8 to 17 mph, with gusts as high as 32 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.
  Tonight: Patchy smoke and showers and thunderstorms likely before 7pm, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 7pm and 8pm, then showers and thunderstorms likely between 8pm and 2am. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 60. West wind 9 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 33 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.
  Sunday: Mostly sunny, with a high near 77. Northwest wind around 15 mph.
  Sunday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 58. West wind 3 to 14 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-18T06:33:00+00:00):
  1. Thunderstorms and heavy rain this afternoon and evening.
  2. Seasonably cool weather and intervals of wildfire smoke expected over the next few days.
  3. Frontal passages bring chances for showers and thunderstorms Tuesday night into Wednesday before high pressure dominates our weather on Thursday and Friday.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 232 AM EDT Saturday... No significant changes.
LAKE (broad waters):
  ..A Lake Wind Advisory Is In Effect: 
  Today: South winds 10 to 15 knots, increasing to 15 to 25 knots. Partly sunny in the morning. A chance of showers in the morning, then showers likely with a chance of thunderstorms in the afternoon. Smoke in the afternoon. Waves 1 to 3 feet.
  Tonight: South winds 15 to 25 knots, becoming northwest. Becoming partly cloudy overnight. Showers likely with a chance of thunderstorms in the evening, then a slight chance of showers overnight. Waves 1 to 3 feet.
  Sunday: Northwest winds 15 to 20 knots, diminishing to 10 to 15 knots. Mostly sunny. Waves 1 to 3 feet.
LAKE GAGE: water 72F, level 96.11 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-18: highs [GFS 76, Euro 82, ICON 82] spread 6F; precip chance [GFS 48%, Euro 100%, ICON 90%]
  2026-07-19: highs [GFS 76, Euro 75, ICON 76] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 28%, Euro 52%, ICON 10%]
  2026-07-20: highs [GFS 82, Euro 79, ICON 82] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 2%, ICON 0%]
AIR: AQI 32 Good (OZONE) — On Saturday, July 18th, a health alert has been issued statewide across Vermont for Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups (USG) concentrations of fine particulate matter (PM25). Please note that PM25 concentrations may occur in the unhealthy range on an hourly basis for many locations in the state, especially in the late morning through afternoon hours.  Smoke from wildfires is expected to return to the New England region from the smoke plume currently stretching from the Ohio Valley to the mid atlantic region.
SUN: rise 2026-07-18T05:25-04:00, set 2026-07-18T20:32-04:00, UV max 6.1
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 76, low 61: Thunderstorms. Highs in the mid 70s and lows in the low 60s.
  high 77, low 56: Plenty of sun. Highs in the upper 70s and lows in the mid 50s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: Another system will bring the chance for showers and thunderstorms Tuesday and Wednesday. A few strong ones aren’t out of the question, so stay tuned for the latest updates. Otherwise, have the umbrella handy if you plan to head out both days. Dry and pleasant weather will then arrive Thursday and Friday with highs in the 70s to low 80s and lows in the 50s.
